title: Remotes, Tracking, Fetch, Pull, and Push · Mastery Check
summary: Synchronize with a shared repository while separating network transfer, local integration, authentication, and authorization decisions. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a remote synchronization trace and collaboration runbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish remote names, URLs, remote-tracking references, local branches, upstreams, and push destinations.
Fetch explicit remotes and refspecs, inspect updates, and compare local work before integrating.
Explain pull as fetch plus configured integration and select merge, rebase, or fast-forward-only deliberately.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Remote Reference Model evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Remote Reference Model assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Local main is 5 commits ahead and one behind origin/main; fetch updates only remote-tracking refs. Compare ranges, choose integration policy, and name exact source/destination before push. Competency probe: Distinguish remote names, URLs, remote-tracking references, local branches, upstreams, and push destinations.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Remote Reference Model has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Remote Reference Model precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Local main is 5 commits ahead and one behind origin/main; fetch updates only remote-tracking refs. Compare ranges, choose integration policy, and name exact source/destination before push. Competency probe: Distinguish remote names, URLs, remote-tracking references, local branches, upstreams, and push destinations.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: origin is a special central repository that Git discovers automatically.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph for the quiz Remote Reference Model case
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology for the quiz Remote Reference Model case
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation for the quiz Remote Reference Model case
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path for the quiz Remote Reference Model case

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Credential Transport: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Local main is 2 commits ahead and one behind origin/main; fetch updates only remote-tracking refs. Compare ranges, choose integration policy, and name exact source/destination before push. Competency probe: Separate HTTPS or SSH transport, credentials, host trust, authorization, signing, and repository policy.
steps.why: Retrieving Credential Transport through the concrete a remote synchronization trace and collaboration runbook case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate HTTPS or SSH transport, credentials, host trust, authorization, signing, and repository policy.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.
